blocking_findings.finding: Missing subprocess timeout: spawn('duckdb', ...) has no timeout, no AbortSignal, no kill timer. A long-running query (e.g., SELECT generate_series(1, 1000000000)) holds a pool slot indefinitely. After 4 hanging queries, the pool is completely blocked for 30s queue timeout intervals.
blocking_findings.repair_path: Add setTimeout(() => proc.kill(), timeoutMs) in execSQL. Default 30s. Wire AbortSignal for Effect fiber cancellation.

blocking_findings.finding: The tool detects 'Failed to spawn duckdb' and produces a Tool.InvalidArgumentsError with user-friendly install guide. But .pipe(Effect.orDie) converts the fail to a defect. The outer tool.ts wrapper applies another orDie. The install guide never reaches the LLM — all errors become opaque defects.
blocking_findings.repair_path: Remove .pipe(Effect.orDie) from the execute function, or handle spawn failures as Effect.fail so the outer tool wrapper error channel returns them.

blocking_findings.finding: Substring matching on blocked function names causes false positives on string literals, column names (load_factor blocks due to 'load'), single-line comments mentioning blocked functions.
blocking_findings.repair_path: Add string literal and single-line comment stripping before analysis. Consider word-boundary matching or function-call pattern detection.

blocking_findings.finding: 30s timeout only covers queue wait time, not execution time. Once a slot opens, there is no execution timeout. Users see 'timed out' but the subprocess continues as an orphan.
blocking_findings.repair_path: Add per-query execution timeout that kills the subprocess. Pool-wide timeout that covers both queue and execution.

non_blocking_findings: Empty SQL string passes schema validation (no minLength on sql parameter)
No subprocess cancellation on Effect fiber cancellation (orphan processes)
COPY FROM not explicitly blocked by application firewall (only -readonly blocks it)
Column aliases like load_shedding or attach_file cause false-positive firewall blocks
